burial.plot_notes: Mount Hope Cemetery Rochester — returning to the family burial ground

institutions.institution_blurb: Attended the University of Rochester, where her great-great-grandfather Asahel Clark Kendrick had been inaugural professor of Greek in 1850, completing the fourth generation of the Hooker-Kendrick family at the institution.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name 'Kendrick' preserves the family's scholarly identity — her grandfather Ryland Morris Kendrick had been a Rochester classics professor and her great-grandfather Asahel Clark Kendrick had founded the Greek program at the university she would herself attend.

narrative_blocks.header: Four generations taught at her alma mater
narrative_blocks.body: Ann Kendrick McNabb attended the University of Rochester — where her great-great-grandfather Asahel Clark Kendrick had been its founding professor of Greek in 1850, her great-grandfather Ryland Morris Kendrick had taught Greek and Latin, and where the family had maintained a continuous scholarly presence across four generations — and named her own grandson Ryland to carry the family lineage forward. She spent her life in Rochester as a civil rights activist, tutor, early childhood advocate, and supporter of refugee families, sustaining the family's commitment to public service that had begun with a Baptist minister's son teaching Greek in 1831.

sources.label: Ann Kendrick McNabb obituary, Rochester, March 2021

bio.photo_notes: Nathaniel Cooper Kendrick (1900-1969), Dean of Bowdoin College 1947-1966

notable.notable_blurb: Dean of Bowdoin College for twenty years, 1947-1966
notable.primary_url_label: Bowdoin College Special Collections — Kendrick Papers

education.notes: Studied at London and Vienna on traveling fellowship 1925-26.

career.notes: Joined as instructor 1926; assistant professor 1928; associate professor 1932; full professor 1946; acting dean 1946; Dean 1947-1966; Frank Munsey Professor of History Emeritus 1959. Honorary doctorate of humane letters 1966.

institutions.institution_blurb: Graduated University of Rochester 1921, the institution his grandfather Asahel Clark Kendrick had helped found in 1850.
Dean of Bowdoin College 1947-1966; Frank Munsey Professor of History Emeritus; papers held at Bowdoin Special Collections M101.

narrative_blocks.header: Dean of Bowdoin College for twenty years
narrative_blocks.body: Nathaniel Cooper Kendrick served as dean of Bowdoin College from 1947 to 1966 — twenty years — having joined the Bowdoin history faculty in 1926 after completing his PhD at Harvard and being named the Frank Munsey Professor of History Emeritus in 1959, making him one of the longest-serving academic administrators in Bowdoin's history. His grandfather Asahel Clark Kendrick had been among the founding faculty of the University of Rochester a century earlier, making this the third generation of the Hooker-Kendrick line to hold a distinguished academic chair.

narrative_blocks.header: Took his doctorate at Harvard before leading Bowdoin
narrative_blocks.body: Nathaniel Cooper Kendrick earned his doctorate at Harvard University in 1930 — having completed his bachelor's at the University of Rochester, where his grandfather had taught Greek for thirty-eight years — and spent his entire career at Bowdoin College, rising from instructor in 1926 to dean of the college in 1947, a position he held for twenty years. His papers, held at the Bowdoin College Special Collections (M101), document his tenure across the Depression, World War II, and the postwar reshaping of liberal arts education.

sources.label: Bowdoin College Special Collections — Nathaniel Cooper Kendrick Papers (M101)

Mount Hope Cemetery Rochester per FA

career.notes: Member of the class of 1889; joined faculty as instructor in classics; taught Greek and Latin. Son of founding professor Asahel Clark Kendrick.

institutions.institution_blurb: Graduated University of Rochester 1889; joined faculty as instructor in classics, following his father Asahel Clark Kendrick.

narrative_blocks.header: Followed his father into the Rochester Greek classroom
narrative_blocks.body: Ryland Morris Kendrick, son of Asahel Clark Kendrick, joined the faculty of the University of Rochester as an instructor in classics and taught Greek and Latin — the second generation of the Kendrick family to hold a chair at the institution his father had helped establish in 1850. His own son Nathaniel Cooper Kendrick would become dean of Bowdoin College, and his granddaughter Ann Kendrick McNabb would return to Rochester as an alumna, sustaining four generations of the Hooker-Kendrick family's connection to her great-great-grandfather's university.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Asahel C. Kendrick (mentions son Ryland)

INST020.name: University of Rochester
INST020.notes: Founded 1850. Merrill Edwards Gates (HD1687) graduated 1870 with honors in Mathematics, Latin, Greek, and the English Essay Prize. The university awarded him an honorary LL.D. in 1882. Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Rochester Asahel Clark Kendrick (I00943) was among the founding faculty (1850), served as inaugural Munro Professor of Greek 1850-1888, first librarian 1850-1866, acting president 1863 and 1877-78. His son Ryland Morris Kendrick (HD4668, class of 1889) taught Greek and Latin on the same faculty. Ryland's son Nathaniel Cooper Kendrick (HD4670) earned B.A. 1921 here before his Harvard doctorate. Nathaniel's daughter Ann Kendrick McNabb (HD4672) also received her B.A. in History here. Kendrick Hall, the first men's dormitory (1913), was named for Asahel.
